inputs.label: standard
inputs.statistic: The odds ratio for developing Type 2 diabetes among participants with a BMI over 30 was 3.2 (95% CI: 2.7-3.8, p < 0.001) compared to participants with a BMI under 25.
inputs.context: A 10-year longitudinal study of 45,000 adults. Baseline diabetes prevalence in the normal-BMI group was 4.5% over the study period.

inputs.label: minimal
inputs.statistic: Median household income rose 2.3% in nominal terms but fell 1.1% in real terms after adjusting for inflation.
inputs.context: Annual economic report covering the past fiscal year.

inputs.label: european
inputs.statistic: The Gini coefficient for income inequality in the EU rose from 0.297 in 2020 to 0.312 in 2025, with the largest increases in countries that experienced the highest post-pandemic inflation.
inputs.context: Eurostat annual income distribution report covering all 27 EU member states. The EU's Gini coefficient has historically ranged between 0.28 and 0.31 over the past two decades.

inputs.label: edge-case
inputs.statistic: The treatment group showed a 200% increase in survival rate compared to placebo (3 of 150 patients survived vs 1 of 150 in the control group), but the absolute risk reduction was only 1.3 percentage points (p=0.62).
inputs.context: A Phase II clinical trial for a rare pediatric cancer. The press release headline reads 'Drug triples survival rate in childhood cancer trial.' The finding did not reach statistical significance.
inputs.audience: Health reporters at a press briefing who need to file within 2 hours

inputs.label: non-english-cultural
inputs.statistic: Japan's total fertility rate fell to 1.20 in 2024, down from 1.26 in 2023, while the absolute number of births dropped below 730,000 for the first time since records began in 1899.
inputs.context: Japan's Ministry of Health annual demographic report. The government's target fertility rate is 1.80. The replacement-level fertility rate is 2.1.
inputs.audience: General international news readers unfamiliar with demographic statistics

inputs.label: different-format
inputs.statistic: The index of consumer sentiment fell from 72.6 to 64.7 in a single month, a decline of 10.9% — the largest one-month drop since the 2008 financial crisis. The expectations subindex fell even more sharply, dropping 14.2%.
inputs.context: University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index, a monthly survey of 500 households. The index is benchmarked to 1966 = 100. It is often cited alongside GDP and unemployment as an indicator of economic direction.
inputs.audience: Listeners of a business podcast with moderate economic literacy

inputs.label: adversarial
inputs.statistic: Crime went up 40% but also the crime rate went down because of more people. The police said violent crime is up but property crime is down and overall crime is basically flat.
inputs.context: A city council press conference about annual crime statistics. The journalist is unsure which figures are correct.

inputs.label: stress-scenario
inputs.statistic: Preliminary data from the WHO indicates a case fatality rate of 3.2% (95% CI: 1.8-5.1%) among confirmed cases, but testing capacity in the affected region is estimated at only 15-20% of symptomatic individuals, and the majority of confirmed cases are hospital-presenting (severe) cases.
inputs.context: An emerging infectious disease outbreak in a densely populated region. The WHO has declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. Reporting is live and there is intense public fear. The 3.2% figure is being compared to COVID-19's early CFR.
inputs.audience: General public during a public health emergency
inputs.publication: Live broadcast news ticker and website
